Chapter: Leader-Friendly Dashboard.
Current document status: Draft.

Section: Leader-Friendly Project Dashboard.

This page is a quick starting point for KBC leaders. It shows what needs attention now, what documents are ready to review, and which committee or body should review each item.

Status labels used on this page:

Draft.
Needs Personnel Review.
Needs Finance Review.
Needs Leadership Review.
Needs Bylaw Review.
Ready to Share.
Approved.
Parking Lot.

Section: 1. Current Status.

KBC is in a Treasurer transition, and interim coverage is helping the church maintain continuity while leaders clarify the longer-term financial operations structure.

On June 28, 2026, the congregation approved adding a paid, part-time Bookkeeper position. The motion tasked Personnel and Nominating Committees to work together to add the position, gave Personnel Committee authority to hire the right candidate, gave Finance Committee responsibility for adjusting the budget to fund this specific operational need, and set a limit of $150 per week, described as $18.75 per hour for approximately 8 hours.

The main work now is to move carefully from authorization to implementation: clarify duties, confirm controls, finalize hiring materials, coordinate the committees named in the motion, and document decisions.

Section: 2. Most Important Next Step.

Confirm the official minutes for the June 28, 2026 motion and make sure the job description, hiring process, supervision plan, compensation, and budget adjustment stay within the authorization already granted.

At the same time, Finance Committee should confirm the financial duties, controls, access, software responsibilities, monthly reporting expectations, and budget impact before Personnel Committee uses final hiring materials.

Section: 3. Top Decisions Needed.

Do the job description, hours, compensation, and budget adjustment stay within the June 28, 2026 motion?
Which duties stay with the elected Treasurer / Financial Officer?
Which duties should be assigned to the Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator?
What Finance Committee direction is needed before the job is posted, interviews begin, or an offer is made?
Which items still need Deacon review, bylaw review, professional review, church vote, or later congregational updates?

Section: 4. Documents Ready For Review.

Table summary.
Document: Leadership Review Packet. Purpose: Align leaders around the situation, proposed structure, decisions, roadmap, and risks.. Owner: Pastor / leadership team. Status: Needs Leadership Review. Review Needed By: Pastor, Deacons, Finance Committee, Personnel Committee. Priority: High. Link: Open.
Document: Project Brief. Purpose: Provide a short orientation to the project purpose and current situation.. Owner: Project lead TBD. Status: Needs Leadership Review. Review Needed By: Pastor, Deacons, Finance Committee, Personnel Committee. Priority: High. Link: Open.
Document: Personnel Committee Packet. Purpose: Help Personnel review the authorized Bookkeeper role and hiring materials.. Owner: Personnel Committee. Status: Needs Personnel Review. Review Needed By: Personnel Committee. Priority: High. Link: Open.
Document: Finance Committee Packet. Purpose: Help Finance review software, financial controls, reimbursement, spending, cards, and budget questions.. Owner: Finance Committee. Status: Needs Finance Review. Review Needed By: Finance Committee. Priority: High. Link: Open.
Document: Monthly Finance Committee Checklist. Purpose: Provide a practical recurring checklist for monthly financial review.. Owner: Finance Committee. Status: Draft. Review Needed By: Finance Committee, Treasurer, Bookkeeper. Priority: High. Link: Open.

Section: 5. Documents Needing Finance Committee Input.
